chunks.en: I usually need to print reports and some meeting notes for my team.
chunks.ja: 私はいつもチームのためにレポートや会議のメモを印刷する必要があります。

chunks.en: When the printer runs out of paper, I open the tray and add more.
chunks.ja: プリンターの紙が切れたら、トレイを開けて紙を足します。

chunks.en: Sometimes I have to wait because other people are printing first.
chunks.ja: ときどき他の人が先に印刷しているので、待たなければなりません。

chunks.en: Last week the printer had a paper jam, so I pulled the paper out slowly.
chunks.ja: 先週プリンターが紙詰まりを起こしたので、ゆっくり紙を引き出しました。

chunks.en: After that, I check the pages and pick up all my printouts.
chunks.ja: その後、ページを確認してすべての印刷物を取ります。

chunks.en: Finally, I take my pages back to my desk and start working again.
chunks.ja: 最後に、印刷物を持って席に戻り、また仕事を始めます。

outline: Opening: what I usually print at the office
Point 1: adding paper when it runs out
Point 2: waiting and fixing a paper jam
Wrap-up: getting pages and going back to my desk

modelTalk.text: I don't print things very often, but sometimes I need to. I usually print reports for meetings, or maybe a form I have to sign. The office printer is near the kitchen, so it's easy to reach. Sometimes the printer runs out of paper. When that happens, I open the tray and put in a new stack. It's simple, and it only takes a minute. Other times I have to wait, because someone is printing first. I just stand there and wait for my turn. Once the paper got stuck inside, and I had to pull it out slowly. That was a little annoying, but I fixed it. When my pages are ready, I pick them up from the tray. Then I check the pages are mine, and I walk back to my desk. That's it. It's a small part of my day.

chunks.en: I usually need to print contracts and meeting handouts before a big meeting.
chunks.ja: 私はたいてい、大事な会議の前に契約書や会議の配布資料を印刷する必要があります。

chunks.en: When the printer runs out of paper, I open the tray and refill it myself.
chunks.ja: プリンターの紙が切れたときは、トレイを開けて自分で紙を補充します。

chunks.en: Sometimes I have to wait in line, or I fix a paper jam.
chunks.ja: ときどき順番待ちをしたり、紙詰まりを直したりしなければなりません。

chunks.en: After that, I grab my pages and take them back to my desk.
chunks.ja: そのあと、印刷物を取って自分の席に持ち帰ります。

chunks.en: In the end, using the office printer is easy once you know the steps.
chunks.ja: 結局のところ、オフィスのプリンターは手順さえ分かれば簡単に使えます。

outline: Opening: what I print at work
Point 1: refill paper when it runs out
Point 2: waiting and fixing a paper jam
Wrap-up: grab pages, it's easy once you know it

modelTalk.text: So, I usually print pretty simple things at work. Most days I print out reports, meeting notes, or an email that my boss wants on paper. When the printer runs out of paper, I don't panic. I just open the tray, grab some sheets from the shelf, and load them in carefully. Sometimes I have to wait because a coworker is printing first. That's fine, so I just chat while I wait. Once there was a paper jam, and I had to pull the paper out slowly so it wouldn't tear. When my pages are ready, I check that they're all mine, and I make sure nothing is missing. Then I pick them up, staple them if I need to, and walk back to my desk. It's a small thing, but it keeps my day moving.
